---
title: "Contact"
---

## Email

The fastest way to reach me is by email. Write to **andrewrosenberg [at] ufl dot edu** — translate the brackets and "dot" into a normal address before sending.

## Prospective students

I receive many inquiries each year about graduate study in UF Political Science. Prospective students should know that **admissions decisions are made by the department, not by individual faculty.** I cannot review individual applications, evaluate fit before you apply, or take meetings with prospective applicants. If you are interested in working with me or any of my colleagues, please indicate it in your statement of purpose. The information below covers what I am asked most often.

**Most importantly, the GRE is required for all graduate applicants to our department.**

### Ph.D. program

- **Application deadline:** December 15.
- **Funding:** Competitive packages including tuition waivers and stipends.
- **Fields:** American Politics, Comparative Politics, International Relations, Political Theory, and Political Methodology. Our department's research clusters include the [Artificial Intelligence Lab](https://aipolsresearch.lab.ufl.edu/), the [East European Politics Group](https://polisci.ufl.edu/the-east-european-politics-group/), the [Election Lab](https://election.lab.ufl.edu/), the [Political Behavior Lab](https://www.hannahalarian.com/lab), and the [Violence, Conflict, and Security Studies Lab](http://vics.lab.ufl.edu).
- **Contacting faculty in advance?** Please note potential advisors in your statement of purpose.
- **Will you review my CV or statement of purpose before I apply?** No. All materials go through the formal admissions review.
- **Full application instructions:** [UF Political Science graduate admissions](https://polisci.ufl.edu/graduate/admissions/).

### M.A. programs

UF Political Science offers a General M.A. in Political Science, an M.A. in International Relations, an M.A. in Political Campaigning, and a combined M.A./J.D. with the UF College of Law. Interdisciplinary certificates are available in Latin American Studies, African Studies, Modern European Studies, Women's Studies, and Global Islam.

- **Fall deadline:** March 15 (General M.A., Political Campaigning, and M.A. in IR).
- **Spring deadline:** November 15 (Political Campaigning only).
- **Funding:** M.A. students are not typically funded by the department.
- **Application materials:** Statement of purpose, CV, transcripts, three letters of recommendation, and GRE scores.
- **Full information:** [UF Political Science graduate admissions](https://polisci.ufl.edu/graduate/admissions/).

## Address

::: {.address-grid}
::: {.address-col}
**Office**\
333 Anderson Hall\
University of Florida\
Gainesville, FL 32611
:::

::: {.address-col}
**Mailing address**\
University of Florida\
Department of Political Science\
234 Anderson Hall\
P.O. Box 117325\
Gainesville, FL 32611
:::
:::

```{=html}
<div class="map-wrap">
<iframe
  src="https://www.openstreetmap.org/export/embed.html?bbox=-82.3450%2C29.6495%2C-82.3390%2C29.6535&layer=mapnik&marker=29.6515774%2C-82.3418860"
  loading="lazy"
  title="Anderson Hall, University of Florida"></iframe>
<p class="map-attribution"><a href="https://www.openstreetmap.org/?mlat=29.6515774&amp;mlon=-82.3418860#map=18/29.6515774/-82.3418860" target="_blank" rel="noopener">View larger map</a> · © OpenStreetMap contributors</p>
</div>
```
